title: Investigate GitHub Pages publication for agent-utils
description:
The GitHub Pages site for agent-utils is not accessible at https://a.skh.am/agent-utils. Need to investigate whether:
1. The site is being published correctly
2. The publication is configured to run on a self-hosted runner
3. The DNS/domain mapping is set up correctly
4. The GitHub Actions workflow is executing successfully

Acceptance criteria:
- Determine if GH Pages is actually being published
- Verify if self-hosted runner is being used and if it's properly configured
- Document any configuration issues found
- Restore access to the site or provide explanation of why it's unavailable

Controller finding from agnt-ctrl while driving app automation: the Pages SPA code was reintegrated to main at 501b817. `gh run list --workflow pages.yml` shows the self-hosted Deploy GitHub Pages workflow triggering, but the newest run 25773551749 was still queued/in progress at observation time and older runs were cancelled by concurrency. `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pages` returned 404, so Pages may not yet be enabled/configured even though the workflow exists. Follow-up should inspect the queued run/runner availability and repository Pages settings/custom domain for https://a.skh.am/agent-utils.

Controller follow-up 2026-05-14: agent-utils .github/workflows/pages.yml does not fully match the android-utils Pages example. Current agent-utils workflow has separate build/deploy jobs, both runs-on: self-hosted, concurrency cancel-in-progress: true, actions/configure-pages@v5 without enablement: true, upload/deploy actions v3/v4, and no explicit linux runner label. Android-utils uses a single deploy job on runs-on: [self-hosted, linux], timeout-minutes: 10, cancel-in-progress: false, stages docs into RUNNER_TEMP with .nojekyll, and calls actions/configure-pages@v6 with enablement: true before upload/deploy @v5. Current external check still returns 404 for https://a.skh.am/agent-utils and gh api repos/{owner}/{repo}/pages still returns 404; latest pages.yml run observed queued. Operator note: regular GitHub-hosted linux runner capacity should be available, so an implementation should consider moving the static Pages job to ubuntu-latest rather than depending on scarce self-hosted runner capacity, unless a repo policy requires self-hosted.
creator: pocket4:cacophony:harry

title: Add configurable Pi package autoupdate preflight extension
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: add an extension for autoupdate settings so that when package updates are available at startup, Pi can automatically run `pi update --extensions` cleanly as part of a preflight phase.

Observed startup excerpt:
Package updates are available. Run pi update
Packages:
- github.com/harryaskham/agent-utils

Requested behavior:
- Add a configurable auto-update/preflight extension, likely building on `extensions/pi-self-update.js`, that detects the same "package updates available" condition before the normal interactive session proceeds.
- When enabled, run `pi update --extensions` cleanly from a safe cwd, with timeout/error reporting consistent with the existing `/update` command.
- Avoid update loops: only run once per startup/check result, handle no-op/no-updates as success, and avoid recursively restarting/reloading forever.
- After successful extension package updates, reload resources/tools or instruct the user when a full restart is required.
- Provide user-facing controls for enabling/disabling autoupdate and for status/help. If Pi does not yet expose first-class extension settings in `/settings`, use a namespaced config convention and document it.
- Add tests covering enabled/disabled behavior, successful update, update failure, and no-update cases without running real `pi update` in tests.

Controller notes: filed from operator startup transcript. Existing `extensions/pi-self-update.js` already provides `/update`, `pi_self_update`, `/reload-tools`, and restart helpers around `pi update --extensions`; this bead should extend that surface into a startup/preflight opt-in rather than duplicating the update runner.

Operator follow-up from Harry, 2026-05-17: settings.json exposure is acceptable for this autoupdate feature. A first-class Pi `/settings` integration is not required for this bead; use a clearly documented namespaced JSON setting plus command/status helpers.

title: Register openai-realtime provider during realtime extension startup
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: when the realtime extension is enabled, it should register the `openai-realtime` provider and `gpt-realtime-2` model immediately during Pi startup, not only lazily when `/rt` commands are called.

Observed startup symptom:
`Warning: No models match pattern "openai-realtime/gpt-realtime-2"`

Requested behavior:
- The realtime extension should call `pi.registerProvider()` during extension factory/startup so the provider/model exist before model pattern resolution, scoped-model checks, and startup warnings.
- `/rt` commands should switch to the realtime provider/model as needed, but should not be the first point where the model becomes known.
- Use the documented extension startup pattern: provider registrations made during the extension factory are queued and flushed before normal startup continues; async factories are awaited before `session_start`/`resources_discover`.
- Add regression coverage that enabling the realtime extension makes `openai-realtime/gpt-realtime-2` visible during startup or `--list-models`, before invoking any `/rt` command.

Controller notes: filed from operator startup transcript. Relevant docs: pi `docs/extensions.md` says async extension factories are awaited before startup and `pi.registerProvider()` calls during factory load are flushed before normal startup; `pi.registerProvider()` is the supported dynamic provider/model registration API.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Add trueDefault model/provider/effort settings guard
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-05-17: add `trueDefault*` settings.json entries for model, provider, and effort/thinking so runtime TUI changes do not permanently overwrite the user's real defaults.

Problem: Pi's built-in TUI controls mutate the standard default settings when the user changes model/provider/effort at runtime. Harry wants to be able to change model at runtime just fine, but have a plugin-owned immutable-by-convention set of true defaults that only changes when settings.json is edited directly.

Requested behavior:
- Support namespaced settings.json entries such as `trueDefaultProvider`, `trueDefaultModel`, and `trueDefaultThinkingLevel` / `trueDefaultEffort` (final names to align with Pi setting terminology; Pi docs currently call the effort setting `defaultThinkingLevel`).
- On Pi load/startup, force-copy the true-default values onto the built-in default provider/model/effort settings before the session uses them.
- On clean Pi shutdown, force-copy the true-default values onto the built-in default provider/model/effort settings again, so any runtime TUI model/effort changes do not persist as the normal defaults.
- Runtime model/provider/effort switching must remain allowed during the session. This is a persistence/default guard, not a runtime lock.
- Do not modify the `trueDefault*` values from TUI/runtime commands; they should change only via direct settings.json edit unless Harry explicitly asks for a command to edit them later.
- Add documentation with an example settings.json block and explain the relationship to `/model`, Ctrl+P, `/settings`, and the requested `/effort` shortcut (`bd-c4a33f`).
- Add tests for startup restoration, clean-shutdown restoration, and runtime model changes remaining possible.

Controller notes: no duplicate open/draft bead was visible during triage. Related request: `bd-c4a33f` adds a quick `/effort` shortcut; this bead covers persistent true-default semantics across model/provider/effort settings.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Implement Rust skill-server utility with MCP stdio meta-tool
description:
Implement a new Rust-based agent-utils utility called `skill-server` (`ss`) anchored around the `mcp-cli` crate, using Tendril as the reference for CLI/MCP stdio structure.

Goal:
- Provide both a CLI and stdio MCP server for dynamic skill/tool discovery without requiring agents to fully reload.
- Expose a single meta-tool / slash entrypoint for agents, conceptually `/ss <domain> <query-or-command>`.
- Example: `/ss web query ...` discovers a registered web-search MCP tool/server on the host and calls it.

Configuration:
- Read host configuration from `.config/ss/config.yaml` by default, with env/flag override if practical.
- Config should define skill search paths and MCP server definitions, e.g. paths to scan for Pi/Cacophony skills plus named MCP stdio servers/commands.
- Config should allow discovery of skills and other host MCP servers, including metadata needed to route a request to a matching server/tool.

Implementation expectations:
- Rust workspace/crate for `skill-server`/`ss`.
- Use or mirror Tendril's `mcp-cli` usage for JSON envelopes, CLI/MCP parity, and `mcp stdio` entrypoint design.
- Keep CLI and MCP execution paths unified.
- Implement a skeleton that builds first, with typed config loading, discovery abstractions, and a minimal meta-tool call path.
- Add tests/smokes for config parsing and MCP/CLI surface where feasible.

Acceptance criteria:
- `ss` (or `skill-server`) has a working CLI help surface.
- `ss mcp stdio` starts a stdio MCP server using mcp-cli-style plumbing.
- `.config/ss/config.yaml` sample/default is documented and supports search paths plus MCP server definitions.
- A minimal `/ss`-style meta request can discover a configured skill/server/tool and return a structured result or clear structured error.
- Build/test instructions are documented.

Filed from agnt-ctrl after Harry requested a new utility skill-server and pointed to Tendril's MCP CLI usage as the example.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl

title: Expose agent-side realtime call and Pulse device controls
description:
Operators currently can start Pi realtime calls through the user-facing `/rt` slash command, and the realtime extension can read Pulse routing from process environment such as `PI_RT_AUDIO_BACKEND=pulse`, `PULSE_SERVER=sgu24:4713`, `PULSE_SOURCE=source.bluetooth`, and `PULSE_SINK=...`. From the agent side, though, there is no first-class tool/API surface for managing a realtime call or selecting Pulse routing at runtime.

Observed in helsinki-cacophony-cluster-ctrl session while trying to satisfy Harry's request: "start a rt call with pulse on sgu24:4713 and device source.bluetooth". Inspection of `extensions/realtime-agent.js` and `docs/realtime-agent.md` showed `/rt start vad|ptt|nolisten`, `/rt backend pulse`, `/rt doctor`, and env/custom-command knobs, but no agent-callable tool for configuring `PULSE_SERVER` / `PULSE_SOURCE` / `PULSE_SINK`, starting/stopping/listening, or reporting call state.

Desired capability:
- Expose agent-side tools/control methods for realtime call lifecycle: start, stop, cancel, status/doctor, audio on/off, listen mode, STT mode.
- Expose safe runtime configuration for Pulse routing: server, source, sink, and/or explicit record/playback commands, ideally scoped to the active Pi session/call rather than requiring Pi process restart.
- Make the tool status clearly report the resolved backend and Pulse endpoints so agents can confirm the requested device is in use.
- Preserve the existing slash-command UX and safety boundaries; do not leak API keys or raw environment secrets in tool output.

Acceptance criteria:
- A managed Pi agent can start a realtime call using Pulse server `sgu24:4713` and source `source.bluetooth` without operator-only slash-command intervention or Pi process restart, assuming credentials and Pulse reachability exist.
- Agents can query status/doctor output and stop/cancel the call via the same agent-side surface.
- Documentation explains both the slash command and agent-side tool workflows.

Filed from helsinki Cacophony controller after Harry requested agent-managed realtime call support.

title: Bug: /rt start vad does not switch Pi to realtime model
description:
Harry observed during live Pulse/RT testing that `/rt start vad` did not switch the active Pi model away from the current text model (gpt55) to `openai-realtime/gpt-realtime-2`. The result was STT-like behaviour: the transcript was delivered to the normal text agent, but the special realtime model/audio path did not receive the audio itself, so full realtime speech behaviour was effectively going somewhere else / not attached to the agent conversation.

Expected behaviour:
- `/rt start vad` (and `/rt`, `/rt ptt`, `/rt nolisten`) should switch Pi to the realtime provider/model before or while opening the realtime session, unless explicitly in STT-only mode.
- `/rt stt ...` should remain transcription-only and should not switch the current model.
- Status/doctor should make it obvious whether the active model is realtime or STT-only.

Observed environment during the same debugging session:
- PI_RT_AUDIO_BACKEND=pulse
- PULSE_SERVER=sgu24.miku-owl.ts.net:4713
- Pulse default source was source.bluetooth, and source.bluetooth was RUNNING.
- Transcripts were reaching the agent, proving microphone capture/transcription was at least partially functioning.

Likely investigation area:
- `extensions/realtime-agent.js::startRealtime(ctx, { listenMode, sttOnly })` around the model-switching path.
- Ensure `startRealtime` verifies `pi.setModel(...)` success and reports a hard/visible error if the realtime model is not found or cannot be selected.
- Add tests covering `/rt start vad` from a non-realtime current model and `/rt stt vad` preserving the current model.

Related beads: bd-36c9e2 (agent-side realtime call and Pulse controls), bd-5d068b (env-style slash/tool args).

title: Add env-style key=value parser for symmetrical slash command and tool args
description:
Harry requested that realtime Pulse routing be runtime-settable both from slash commands and from the agent tool view once agent-side realtime tools are exposed.

Observed semantics check:
- Pi extension docs show pi.registerCommand(name, { handler(args, ctx) }) receives a raw args string; I did not find a built-in reusable key=value / env-style parser for slash-command args in the documented Pi command API.
- Pi tools already use structured Type.Object parameters via pi.registerTool(...), which is the right canonical shape for agent-side tool arguments.

Desired behaviour:
- Add or reuse a shared parser/helper for env-like command syntax such as /rt backend=pulse source=source.bluetooth sink=...
- Order must not matter: /rt source=source.bluetooth backend=pulse should be equivalent.
- Spaces should delimit tokens but not be semantic otherwise.
- Support shell/env-like quoted values, e.g. key="value in quotes", including useful escaping rules and clear errors for malformed input.
- Realtime slash commands should map parsed key/value args into the same normalized parameter object that the agent-side realtime tool uses, so command UX and tool UX stay symmetrical.
- Preserve existing positional forms where sensible (/rt start vad, /rt backend pulse) while documenting precedence and ambiguity handling.
- Realtime should include runtime-settable Pulse fields at minimum: backend, pulse server, source, sink; changes should affect newly spawned mic/playback processes and status/doctor output should report the resolved routing without leaking secrets.
- Prefer an existing Pi parser/semantics if one exists in a newer API; otherwise implement the helper in agent-utils as shared library code and document it as the convention for future command/tool pairs.

Related existing bead: bd-36c9e2 (Expose agent-side realtime call and Pulse device controls). This bead focuses on the shared env-style arg parsing and command/tool symmetry needed by that work.

Filed from l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9 at Harry's request after Pulse Bluetooth mic testing discussion.

title: Add bounded autoreconnect for realtime WebSocket timeouts
description:
Harry observed during live realtime/Pulse testing that the realtime connection times out easily and then does not automatically reconnect. The `/rt` UI was still showing the restore/previous-model state (e.g. ↩ litellm-openai/gpt-5.5), but once the realtime WebSocket/session timed out the extension did not retry or recover automatically.

Expected behaviour:
- For realtime call/STT modes, transient WebSocket timeouts or close events should trigger bounded automatic reconnect when the user has not explicitly turned realtime off.
- Reconnect should preserve intended mode where safe: full realtime vs STT-only, audio on/off, listen mode (vad/ptt/nolisten), voice/backend/reasoning, and previous-model restore target.
- Reconnect attempts should be bounded/backoff-based and visible in `/rt-status` / `/rt-doctor` (last disconnect reason, retry count, next retry delay).
- Explicit `/rt-off`, `/rt stop` close, or user cancellation should disable autoreconnect.
- If reconnect ultimately fails, surface a clear error and leave the session in a consistent stopped state.

Acceptance tests / coverage ideas:
- Simulate a realtime WebSocket close/timeout while `/rt start vad` is active and assert reconnect is attempted.
- Simulate close after explicit `/rt-off` and assert no reconnect occurs.
- Verify diagnostics report last disconnect/reconnect state.

Related bugs/features: bd-5896e5 (`/rt start vad` does not switch Pi to realtime model), bd-36c9e2 (agent-side realtime call and Pulse controls).

Filed from l7fipzlhc4u4d0t9 at Harry's request during live RT/Pulse testing.

description:
ms-dev SSH/Tailscale reachability is flapping and currently blocks stable live work-app refreshes for Slack, Outlook, Calendar, and Teams via the PowerShell/CDP bridge.

- Direct probes have shown both `ssh: connect to host ms-dev port 22: Connection timed out` and `Connection timed out during banner exchange` / `Connection to <tailscale-ip> port 22 timed out`.
- Tailscale ping has alternated between no reply and intermittent DERP replies.
- app_automation_msdev_cdp_refresh now preflights SSH before copy/run and classifies the failure as connect_timeout when SSH emits timeout text.
- 2026-05-13T11:40Z: Retried through the ms-dev PowerShell/CDP route with sshConnectTimeoutSeconds=5 and preflightAttempts=3. All three preflight attempts still failed before copy/run. Manifest reported preflight_failed for all six work-app actions with failureErrorKinds=connect_timeout=6. - 2026-05-13T11:48Z: After bd-303dc2 landed, a three-attempt preflight retry still failed. One separate verbose direct SSH probe briefly authenticated and accepted `true` but hung until the wrapper killed it, showing that the preflight command itself can hang after auth.
- 2026-05-13T11:55Z: With preflightAttempts=0 (skip preflight) the refresh reached the copy stage but scp still failed with connect_timeout for all six work-app actions. Manifest reported copy_failed with failureErrorKinds=connect_timeout=6.
- 2026-05-13T12:14Z: With preflightAttempts=0 and scriptTransfer=inline, the bridge bypassed scp and tried to run PowerShell via `pwsh -EncodedCommand`, but the SSH run stage still failed with connect_timeout for all six work-app actions. Impact:
- Fresh Slack/Outlook/Calendar/Teams pulls cannot reliably reach Windows PowerShell/Chrome CDP.
- Preserved stale-aware snapshots remain available, but freshness will degrade until SSH/Tailscale reachability is restored.

Do not close this bead until ms-dev SSH/Tailscale reachability is stable enough for fresh app_automation_msdev_cdp_refresh runs to reach copy/run and pull current work-app snapshots.

title: Add kitty graphics-backed Pi theme package
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: investigate/implement shipping a Pi theme as part of the agent-utils Pi plugin/package, backed by the existing kitty graphics library with a TypeScript implementation.

Context / feasibility:
- Pi packages can ship themes: package.json supports a `pi.themes` entry and conventional `themes/` directories; packages can also ship extensions alongside themes.
- Pi themes themselves are JSON color-token definitions, but a theme+extension combination can provide richer custom rendering using the extension APIs.
- caco-tui has good Rust kitty graphics examples, but the Rust crate is not currently FFI/linkable/callable from Pi TypeScript, so implement a TypeScript renderer for now rather than trying to link the Rust crate.

Requested direction:
- Build a Pi package/extension/theme combination that uses agent-utils' kitty graphics protocol/library to render freeform graphical UI affordances.
- Prototype a small TypeScript renderer that can render lines/borders/styles into actual byte/image form suitable for kitty graphics display and Unicode placeholder placement.
- Use kitty Unicode placeholder characters to display graphical output at theme/UI locations, e.g. replacing simple ASCII prompt enclosure lines like `-----` with rendered graphics.
- Explore graphical/translucent/gradient borders around tables, agent/code blocks, or other message blocks.
- Keep the implementation in TypeScript for Pi; consult caco-tui Rust examples only as a reference for protocol/rendering behavior.
- Package any theme assets through `themes/` or package.json `pi.themes`, and any runtime behavior through the Pi extension package entries.
- Add docs/examples explaining how to install/select the theme and how the extension cooperates with the theme.
- Add tests for renderer byte output/protocol generation and for package manifest discovery where practical.

Important constraints:
- Do not globally clear/delete kitty graphics images; coordinate with bd-f89780's scoped image ownership/cleanup work.
- Avoid redrawing/destroying the Caco/Pi UI; all image placement/deletion should be scoped to the extension-owned images/placeholders.
- Preserve existing terminal/tmux placeholder behavior.

Controller triage notes:
- Pi docs confirm packages can ship themes via package.json `pi.themes` or conventional `themes/` directories.
- Duplicate searches for `kitty theme`, `unicode placeholder theme`, and `graphical borders` found no matching bead across all statuses.
- Main-history keyword check found prior kitty graphics/image preview work, but not this theme-backed graphical rendering feature.
- Depends on bd-f89780 because scoped image ownership/cleanup is prerequisite safety for using kitty graphics to decorate core UI regions.

description: Profile audit finding from pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl reified instructions. The project-controller profile for this same agent says it is a persistent controller, must not pick up worker tasks or implement application code, must not call caco agent complete, and should use reintegrate only for controller-owned operational changes. There is also recurring-task ambiguity: generic guidance says to use daemon-managed caco cron for recurring tasks, while project-controller/Pi guidance says per-controller self-check loops should stay inside the Pi runtime and not rely on daemon-global cron. Impact: persistent controllers receive contradictory lifecycle/startup/scheduling instructions and may mistakenly behave as bead workers. Please triage/profile-materialization so persistent controllers get role-specific lifecycle guidance without worker-only completion/hydration requirements and clarify scheduling precedence.

title: Keep realtime summary context out of spoken user turns
description: Live /rt summary mode spoke the actual compaction summary and also tripped summary context overflow/auto-compaction failures. Summary mode currently forwarded the compact summary as a realtime user conversation item, so the model treated it as speech content. Move summary context into session instructions/system context instead, forward only the current turn/audio trigger, cap fallback summary size so summary mode cannot exceed realtime context, and ensure auto-compaction/realtime errors don't speak summaries. Also ensure STT-only transcript injection queues with followUp while the agent is busy so transcription during active work doesn't throw 'Agent is already processing'.

title: Realtime tool-call ID not found after tool invocation
description:
Observed issue: when a realtime model running inside the agent harness makes any tool call, the environment returns an error like 'tool_call_id not found in conversation' immediately after the call. This appears to be specific to the realtime/harness context rather than the user command itself. Impact: tool calls fail and cannot be relied on in-session. 

Repro notes: run a realtime model inside the agent harness and invoke any tool; after the tool call, the system reports the missing tool_call_id error.

Expected: tool calls succeed and are tracked; no missing tool_call_id errors.
Actual: error indicates tool_call_id not found.

Please investigate tool-call tracking/state reconciliation for realtime sessions in the agent harness.

title: Use longer wrapper timeout for ms-dev SSH preflight
description: Post-landing validation for bd-ce1ea4 still produced `preflight_failed/errorKind=command_timeout` because the app wrapper killed `ssh ... true` at 13s before ssh emitted its own `connect to host ms-dev port 22: Connection timed out`. A direct bounded SSH probe with a longer outer timeout did emit the connect-timeout text. Increase the ms-dev SSH preflight process timeout budget (single preflight per refresh, acceptable to wait longer) so `app_automation_msdev_cdp_refresh` reliably captures SSH connect/banner failures as connect_timeout instead of command_timeout. Filed from live Slack/Outlook/Calendar/Teams drive.

title: Improve kitty graphics multiviewer navigation and scoped cleanup
description:
Operator request from Harry via agnt-ctrl, 2026-04-29: improve the Pi kitty graphics image display extension/multiviewer.

Requested behavior:
- Add controls/commands for the multiviewer to move through images explicitly: e.g. /kitty-show-next and /kitty-show-prev.
- Add a timed cycling mode: e.g. /kitty-start-cycle 5 to cycle images every 5 seconds and /kitty-stop-cycle to stop. If there is an existing command naming convention, follow it, but provide equivalent next/previous/start/stop-cycle behavior.
- Make the multiviewer UI expose these controls clearly enough for users.
- Fix image cleanup/deletion: the plugin appears to be killing all kitty graphics images in the entire terminal. When run inside caco this redraws/destroys the entire UI. The extension must only manage/delete the kitty images it created.

Implementation guidance:
- Track image IDs / placements / namespaces owned by this extension, and scope clear/delete operations to those owned resources only.
- Avoid global kitty graphics clear/delete sequences that affect unrelated images or the caco UI.
- Preserve existing tmux/placeholder placement behavior and current preview commands.
- Add/update tests and docs for navigation, cycle controls, and scoped cleanup behavior.
- Verify with the project’s normal test suite or targeted tests if the full suite is not practical.

Controller triage notes:
- Existing closed bead bd-74597a covers the original Pi kitty graphics extension, not these multiviewer/cycle/scoped-cleanup changes.
- Search/main-history check did not find an already-landed multiviewer cycle/scoped cleanup change on main before filing this bead.
creator: pocket4:agent-utils:pocket4-agent-utils-agnt-ctrl
